_note: SELF-HOST repurposing of the RE-RANK 08 schema (D21): each entry = one remaining PROMPT-BUILD/CR, not a product slice. done_sentinel = the disk path whose presence+validity == that build shipped (orchestrator STEP-0 derived-state key; D14/D20 analog). roadmap_version 5 = CR-003 re-rank (operator-directed RE-RANK pass, 2026-06-11): inserts W1b-REGISTRY-ENRICH after W6-REAUTHOR-04BUILD (position 8), shifting W6-REAUTHOR-REST/W7/W9/W8 down one. SCOPED TO THE CR-002 deterministic-spine wave + the CR-003 fix; prior delivered waves (greenfield 10, bugfix 8) live on disk (D20), not re-listed. Disk is source of truth — this ledger is informational ordering, not the truth the loop derives from. W0-CANON-CR shipped; W1-W5 + W6-REAUTHOR-04BUILD sentinels present on disk; derived frontier = W1b-REGISTRY-ENRICH. W1b appended by the CR-003 RE-RANK pass: W6-REAUTHOR-REST clean-room (03-hld) surfaced a value-parity drift (re-authored DERIVE-COMPONENTS 5-box vs golden/original 6-box) rooted in a thin W1 registry (0 description field-docs, violates D24 §1; loose element types let a non-conformant shape validate-clean). W1b enriches the registry to D24 fidelity + re-freezes, re-triggering W6-REAUTHOR-04BUILD re-verify (shipped, same gap) + unblocking W6-REAUTHOR-REST (held scratch at prompts/03-hld/.scratch/, un-promoted, resume-safe). Anti-thrash (RM6): W2-W5 shipped + unaffected by registry descriptions → left in base order; only the dependency-legal insertion of W1b at the frontier moves later entries. Hand-maintained under operator override; the literal greenfield RE-RANK prompt (product-slice projection) does not apply to this wave-build repurposing.

completed.name: deterministic-machine canon CR (intake)
completed.unit: .aprd/change-requests/CR-002.md + aprd.v5 + ADRs 0024-0027 + skeleton.lock v5 + all re-signed locks

remaining_sequence.name: schema-registry + validate.mjs + selftest
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: schemas/schemas.lock + tools/det/validate.mjs + both-directions selftest green (reference conforms; planted bad-shape fails). Externalizes every output schema (R9/D24); validator enforces conformance (R10). NOTE (CR-003): the v1 registry was built THIN — schemas carry 0 description field-docs (D24 §1 violation) + loose element types; W1b-REGISTRY-ENRICH fixes this.

remaining_sequence.name: io-manifest + resolve.mjs + selftest
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: io/io.lock + tools/io/resolve.mjs + both-directions selftest green (right state->right set; planted-wrong-state caught). Read-graph code-owned (R11/D25); prompts declare no inputs.

remaining_sequence.name: graph-lint: gen contracts.json, PR2 check
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: graph-lint.mjs green + generated contracts.json matches frozen .hld/skeleton/contracts.json. PR2 auto-checked by registry-id equality (R14); chain generated not hand-written. Uses registry IDS (not descriptions) -> unaffected by W1b.

remaining_sequence.name: det/ compute modules + selftests
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: all 6 modules + per-module both-directions selftests. verdict-from-count / route-from-axes / topo-sort / monotonic-id / set-bijection / mechanical-prefill computed by code (R12/D26).

remaining_sequence.name: Tier-1 emitters; retire those prompts
remaining_sequence.unit: tools/det/emit/*.mjs + Tier-1 prompts retired (sentinel + components.json note kept)
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: tools/det/emit/*.mjs (baseline-map, build-plan, derive-tests, verify-output, ...) emitter both-directions vs _fixtures + Tier-1 prompts retired (R13/D26/D27). Role keeps sentinel + components.json ownership note (D1).

remaining_sequence.name: re-author 04-build prompts (drop schema+inputs)
remaining_sequence.rationale: Discriminator: 04-build prompts carry NO inputs: block + NO ## Output schema section (skeleton v5); outputs[].schema = registry id. Verify clean-room both-directions; VALUE parity vs pre-CR golden (regression-guard). Biggest payoff (26-30 input lines each). SHIPPED, but CR-003 blast radius: carries the same thin-registry gap (build-record.schema.json = 0 descriptions) -> W1b re-verifies VALUE parity against the enriched registry (no re-author needed; prompts already inputs-less).

remaining_sequence.name: enrich schema registry to D24 fidelity (descriptions + tight constraints) + re-freeze
remaining_sequence.unit: schemas/**/*.schema.json (description per property + tightened element shapes) + schemas.lock re-signed + tools/det/validate.mjs selftest tightened
remaining_sequence.rationale: CR-003 (operator-directed, surfaced by the W6-REAUTHOR-REST clean-room gate). Discriminator: schemas.lock version bumped (>1) AND every schema property carries `description` (ported from the pre-CR prompt inline `## Output schema` // field-docs, P5/B11 — git history, not re-invented) AND loose {type:array|object} on element-bearing fields (seam_realization, requirements_landed, entities_owned, edges, ...) tightened so a non-conformant shape FAILS + validator both-directions selftest green (pre-CR thin schema PASSES a planted bad shape; enriched schema FAILS it). Root cause = W1 under-implemented D24 §1 (NOT a W6 defect — the re-author is mechanically correct, merely exposes the latent gap). Re-triggers W6-REAUTHOR-04BUILD re-verify (shipped, same gap) + unblocks W6-REAUTHOR-REST. NEVER re-add an inline schema to a prompt (violates D24) — the faithful home is the registry.

remaining_sequence.name: re-author 03/02/01/00 prompts, per phase
remaining_sequence.rationale: Per-phase, same bar as W6-04build: no inputs:/schema block, outputs[].schema id, clean-room both-directions, VALUE parity. One self-slice pass per phase. HELD (CR-003): 03-hld scratch authored + discriminator-clean at prompts/03-hld/.scratch/ but NOT promoted — clean-room found value-parity drift rooted in the thin registry. Resumes after W1b: re-verify the held 03-hld scratch (both-directions, VALUE parity) against the enriched registry, then gate + promote; then 02/01/00.

remaining_sequence.name: orchestrator STEP-4.1 swap: resolve+prefill+compute
remaining_sequence.rationale: Discriminator: STEP-4.1 calls resolve(role,state) + prefill + compute + validate around the LLM fill (replaces parse-frontmatter). Gate = full self-host loop drains _fixtures/ clean e2e (regression-guard). Lands LAST among consumers (code + prompts proven first).

remaining_sequence.name: reconcile ALL accumulated lint + ECONOMY-AUDIT findings across the re-authored corpus
remaining_sequence.unit: prompts/**/*.md lint-clean + ECONOMY-AUDIT-clean (both-directions) + prompts/_economy-audit.md Rule-2 / lint-profile calibration where a finding is a dual-mode false-positive
remaining_sequence.rationale: Terminal sweep. Every prompt's lint + ECONOMY-AUDIT residue deferred per-wave during W6/W7 (defer-not-chase, avoids over-stripping the standalone-PART-A/B dual-mode design) is resolved to EXACTLY ONE of: (a) prompt DELETE/REWRITE — genuine AB1/AB7 debt; (b) prompts/_economy-audit.md Rule-2 calibration — a dual-mode standalone-Part restatement is sanctioned, NOT a duplicate (the auditor mis-fires, fix the auditor not the prompt, routes_to:canon); (c) lint-profile threshold note in the stack profile. done_sentinel .roadmap/09-economy-reconcile.json records per-prompt lint verdict:clean + economy-audit verdict:clean across the whole corpus + a both-directions proof (planted-bloat copy still BLOCKED). Discriminator: zero prompt carries an unresolved blocking lint/audit finding AND the report's both-directions proof holds. NEVER ADD an instruction to silence a finding (AB9 keystone).

remaining_sequence.name: pack gate: wire all selftests, re-skin, ship
remaining_sequence.rationale: Compound: pack.mjs runs all selftests (validator + resolver + graph-lint + det modules + emitters + economy-lint) green; dist/adp-v<ver>.tgz ships. Lands AFTER W9 so the corpus is economy-clean in the tarball. No tarball if any selftest mis-proves. Validator selftest now includes W1b's tightened both-directions proof.

coverage.scope_note: Scoped to the CR-002 deterministic-machine wave + the CR-003 registry-fidelity fix. Prior delivered waves (greenfield, bugfix) are not tracked here; their done-state is on disk (D20).
